
# 📊 Custom APEX DATA EXPORT for Hierarchy Table
> Advanced Excel Export with Column Grouping and Styling

**Location**: ⚡ Processing > Process > PL/SQL > Custom APEX DATA EXPORT for Heirarchy Table Page Processing > When Button Pressed - Export 1

## 📋 Table of Contents
1. [Table Definition](#1-table-definition)
2. [Sample Data](#2-sample-data)
3. [Export Process](#3-export-process)

## 1. Table Definition
> 🏗️ Customer Information Table Structure
```sql
CREATE TABLE "T_CUSTOMERS" 
(   
    "CUSTOMER_ID" NUMBER GENERATED BY DEFAULT ON NULL AS IDENTITY 
        MINVALUE 1 
        MAXVALUE 9999999999999999999999999999 
        INCREMENT BY 1 
        START WITH 1 
        CACHE 20 
        NOORDER  
        NOCYCLE  
        NOKEEP  
        NOSCALE  
        NOT NULL ENABLE, 
    "NAME" VARCHAR2(50), 
    "ADDRESS" VARCHAR2(100), 
    "CITY" VARCHAR2(50), 
    "STATE" VARCHAR2(50), 
    "ZIPCODE" VARCHAR2(10), 
    "EMAIL" VARCHAR2(100), 
    "PHONE" VARCHAR2(20), 
    PRIMARY KEY ("CUSTOMER_ID") USING INDEX ENABLE
);
```

## 2. Sample Data
> 📝 Test Data Insert Script
```sql
INSERT INTO t_customers (name, address, city, state)
SELECT name, address, city, state
FROM (
    SELECT 'Name 12' AS name, '123 Fake Street Apt 12' AS address, 'City 12' AS city, 'State 12' AS state FROM dual UNION ALL
    SELECT 'Name 13', '123 Fake Street Apt 13', 'City 13', 'State 13' FROM dual UNION ALL
    SELECT 'Name 14', '123 Fake Street Apt 14', 'City 14', 'State 14' FROM dual UNION ALL
    SELECT 'Name 15', '123 Fake Street Apt 15', 'City 15', 'State 15' FROM dual UNION ALL
    SELECT 'Name 16', '123 Fake Street Apt 16', 'City 16', 'State 16' FROM dual UNION ALL
    SELECT 'Name 17', '123 Fake Street Apt 17', 'City 17', 'State 17' FROM dual UNION ALL
    SELECT 'Name 18', '123 Fake Street Apt 18', 'City 18', 'State 18' FROM dual UNION ALL
    SELECT 'Name 19', '123 Fake Street Apt 19', 'City 19', 'State 19' FROM dual UNION ALL
    SELECT 'Name 20', '123 Fake Street Apt 20', 'City 20', 'State 20' FROM dual
);
```

## 3. Export Process
> 📤 PL/SQL Export Implementation

### 🔧 Process Features
- 📅 Dynamic timestamp in filename
- 📊 Column grouping (Customer Details & Address)
- 🎨 Custom header styling
- 📑 Excel XLSX format
- ⚠️ Error handling

```sql
DECLARE
    l_context apex_exec.t_context; 
    l_export  apex_data_export.t_export;
    lTitle varchar2(250);
    lPrintConfig apex_data_export.t_print_config;  
    lColumns apex_data_export.t_columns;
    lColumnGroups apex_data_export.t_column_groups;
    lCGCustomerDetails pls_integer;
    lCGCustomerAddress pls_integer;
    lFileName varchar2(250);
BEGIN
    -- 📑 Set export title
    lTitle := 'Customer Information Export';

    -- 📅 Generate filename with timestamp
    lFileName := 'Excel-Export' || '-' || TO_CHAR(SYSDATE, 'DD.MM.YYYY HH24:MI:SS') || '.xlsx';

    -- 🔍 Query context setup
    l_context := apex_exec.open_query_context(
        p_location    => apex_exec.c_location_local_db,
        p_sql_query   => q'[
            SELECT 
                CUSTOMER_ID,
                NAME,
                ADDRESS,
                CITY,
                STATE,
                ZIPCODE,
                EMAIL,
                PHONE
            FROM 
                T_CUSTOMERS
        ]'
    );
                
    -- 🎨 Configure print settings
    lPrintConfig := apex_data_export.get_print_config(
        p_page_header => lTitle,
        p_page_header_font_color      => 'Black',
        p_page_header_font_size       => 14,
        p_page_header_font_weight     => apex_data_export.c_font_weight_bold               
    );
    
    -- 📊 Define column groups
    apex_data_export.add_column_group(
        p_column_groups => lColumnGroups,
        p_idx => lCGCustomerDetails,
        p_name => 'Customer Details'
    );
    
    apex_data_export.add_column_group(
        p_column_groups => lColumnGroups,
        p_idx => lCGCustomerAddress,
        p_name => 'Customer Address'
    );
    
    -- 👤 Customer Details columns
    apex_data_export.add_column(
        p_columns => lColumns,
        p_name => 'CUSTOMER_ID',
        p_heading => 'Customer ID',
        p_column_group_idx => lCGCustomerDetails
    );
    
    apex_data_export.add_column(
        p_columns => lColumns,
        p_name => 'NAME',
        p_heading => 'Name',
        p_column_group_idx => lCGCustomerDetails
    );
    
    -- 📫 Customer Address columns
    apex_data_export.add_column(
        p_columns => lColumns,
        p_name => 'ADDRESS',
        p_heading => 'Address',
        p_column_group_idx => lCGCustomerAddress
    );
    
    apex_data_export.add_column(
        p_columns => lColumns,
        p_name => 'CITY',
        p_heading => 'City',
        p_column_group_idx => lCGCustomerAddress
    );
    
    apex_data_export.add_column(
        p_columns => lColumns,
        p_name => 'STATE',
        p_heading => 'State',
        p_column_group_idx => lCGCustomerAddress
    );
    
    apex_data_export.add_column(
        p_columns => lColumns,
        p_name => 'ZIPCODE',
        p_heading => 'Zip Code',
        p_column_group_idx => lCGCustomerAddress
    );
    
    apex_data_export.add_column(
        p_columns => lColumns,
        p_name => 'EMAIL',
        p_heading => 'Email',
        p_column_group_idx => lCGCustomerAddress
    );
    
    apex_data_export.add_column(
        p_columns => lColumns,
        p_name => 'PHONE',
        p_heading => 'Phone',
        p_column_group_idx => lCGCustomerAddress
    );

    -- 📤 Generate and download export
    l_export := apex_data_export.export (
        p_context   => l_context,
        p_print_config => lPrintConfig,
        p_columns => lColumns,
        p_column_groups => lColumnGroups,
        p_format    => apex_data_export.c_format_xlsx,
        p_file_name => lFileName
    );

    apex_exec.close( l_context );
    apex_data_export.download( p_export => l_export );

EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        apex_exec.close( l_context );
        RAISE;
END;
```

## 📝 Implementation Notes
1. **Column Groups** 🔍
   - Customer Details (ID, Name)
   - Customer Address (Address, City, State, etc.)

2. **Export Features** ✨
   - Dynamic filename generation
   - Grouped columns in Excel
   - Custom header formatting
   - Error handling
   - Automatic download

3. **Process Setup** ⚙️
   - Triggered by button press
   - Downloads Excel file
   - Includes timestamp in filename

## ⚠️ Important Considerations
- Ensure proper table structure exists
- Check for sufficient data
- Handle large data sets appropriately
- Test download functionality
- Verify column grouping in Excel output

## 🔄 Dependencies
- APEX_EXEC package
- APEX_DATA_EXPORT package
- Excel support in browser
- Valid table structure
